1→# Significa Website: Documentation
     2→
     3→Reference documentation for the Significa website project. For AI agent instructions, see `CLAUDE.md` in the project root.
     4→
     5→| File | What it covers |
     6→|------|---------------|
     7→| `01-PROJECT-BOOTSTRAP.md` | Stack, commands, project structure, key principles |
     8→| `02-CONTENT-SCHEMA.md` | Content collections, Zod schemas, relationships, draft system |
     9→| `03-MDX-COMPONENTS.md` | Custom components for use inside MDX files |
    10→| `04-MEDIA-ASSETS.md` | S3 + CDN pipeline, MediaImage/MediaVideo, image transform parameters |
    11→| `05-RULES.md` | Do's, don'ts, build-time validation, common mistakes |
    12→| `06-ASTRO-REFERENCE.md` | Astro framework deep reference (components, collections, islands, transitions) |
    13→| `07-TEAM-DECISIONS.md` | Team discussion log and architectural decisions |
    14→| `08-DESIGN-TOKENS.md` | CSS custom properties: spacing, typography, colors, breakpoints |
    15→| `09-SEO-ACCESSIBILITY.md` | SEO checklist and accessibility baseline |
    16→
    17→## Quick Reminders
    18→
    19→1. **Static site.** No server, no API, no database. HTML generated at build time.
    20→2. **Astro v5 (latest stable).** Astro 6 is in beta — upgrade when it reaches stable. Stay on latest v5 until then.
    21→3. **Content Collections are the database.** Use `getCollection()` and `getEntry()`. Never `import.meta.glob`.
    22→4. **References use `reference()`.** Build-time validated cross-collection links. Broken slugs break the build, not production.
    23→5. **Media via S3 + self-hosted CDN.** Uploaded via asset manager, served from `https://cdn.significa.co` with sharp-based image transforms.
    24→6. **Astro components first.** React only for interactive islands.
    25→7. **When unsure, read the Astro docs.** Do not guess. https://docs.astro.build

1→# Project Bootstrap
     2→
     3→## Stack
     4→
     5→- **Astro v5** (latest stable): static site generator, zero JS by default. Astro 6 is in beta (requires Node 22+, Zod 4, removes legacy APIs) — do not upgrade until stable.
     6→- **MDX**: markdown with custom components for case studies and rich content
     7→- **TypeScript**: strict mode, no `any`
     8→- **Content Collections**: Astro's built-in content layer with Zod schemas
     9→- **React**: only for interactive islands (mobile nav, form validation). Keep it minimal.
    10→- **pnpm**: package manager
    11→- **Cloudflare Pages**: static deploy target
    12→
    13→## Init
    14→
    15→```bash
    16→pnpm create astro@latest significa-website -- --template minimal --typescript strict
    17→cd significa-website
    18→pnpm astro add mdx react
    19→```
    20→
    21→## Project Structure
    22→
    23→```
    24→src/
    25→├── components/
    26→│   ├── mdx/                  # Components available inside MDX files
    27→│   │   ├── media-image.astro
    28→│   │   ├── media-video.astro
    29→│   │   ├── comparison-block.astro
    30→│   │   ├── metrics.astro
    31→│   │   ├── project-cross-sell.astro
    32→│   │   └── components.ts      # Centralized MDX component registry
    33→│   ├── ui/                   # Reusable UI components
    34→│   ├── header.astro
    35→│   ├── footer.astro
    36→│   └── seo.astro             # SEO meta tags component
    37→├── content.config.ts         # Collection schemas (the "database")
    38→├── content/
    39→│   ├── projects/             # .mdx per project case study
    40→│   ├── blog/                 # .md or .mdx per blog post
    41→│   ├── labs/                 # .mdx per open source project
    42→│   ├── pages/                # .mdx per misc page (about, services, etc.)
    43→│   ├── highlights/           # .yaml per homepage highlight
    44→│   ├── clients/              # .yaml per client (logo strip)
    45→│   ├── testimonials/         # .yaml per testimonial
    46→│   └── awards/               # .yaml per award
    47→├── layouts/
    48→│   └── base.astro            # Main HTML layout with SEO component
    49→├── pages/
    50→│   ├── index.astro           # Homepage
    51→│   ├── who-we-are.astro      # About page (hybrid: Astro page, not MDX)
    52→│   ├── what-we-do.astro      # Services page (hybrid)
    53→│   ├── work-with-us.astro    # Contact page (hybrid)
    54→│   ├── blog/
    55→│   │   ├── index.astro       # Blog listing
    56→│   │   ├── [slug].astro      # Blog post detail
    57→│   │   └── rss.xml.ts        # RSS feed
    58→│   ├── projects/
    59→│   │   ├── index.astro       # Projects listing
    60→│   │   └── [slug].astro      # Project detail
    61→│   ├── labs/
    62→│   │   ├── index.astro       # Labs listing
    63→│   │   └── [slug].astro      # Lab detail
    64→│   ├── 404.astro             # Custom 404 page
    65→│   └── [...slug].astro       # Catch-all for misc content pages
    66→├── styles/
    67→│   ├── global.css            # Entry point: imports reset + tokens, base styles
    68→│   ├── reset.css             # CSS reset
    69→│   └── tokens.css            # Design tokens (colors, typography, layout, transitions)
    70→└── lib/
    71→    ├── collections.ts        # Helper functions for content queries
    72→    ├── cdn.ts                # CDN URL helpers and srcset generation
    73→    ├── seo.ts                # Structured data generation (JSON-LD)
    74→    └── content-errors.ts     # Build-time error formatting
    75→public/
    76→├── favicon.svg
    77→├── fonts/
    78→└── robots.txt
    79→# Images/videos served via S3 + CDN (cdn.significa.co) (see 04-MEDIA-ASSETS.md)
    80→```
    81→
    82→### Hybrid page architecture
    83→
    84→Some pages are hardcoded Astro files (`who-we-are.astro`, `what-we-do.astro`, `work-with-us.astro`) because they have complex layouts that don't fit the MDX pattern. Other pages (about, services, etc.) live as MDX in `src/content/pages/` and are rendered by the catch-all `[...slug].astro`.
    85→
    86→The reserved routes list in `[...slug].astro` prevents collisions between the two approaches.
    87→
    88→## Key Principles
    89→
    90→1. **No database.** Content lives in MDX/YAML files with typed frontmatter. Content Collections are the database.
    91→2. **No client-side JS by default.** Astro renders everything at build time. Only add `client:load` or `client:visible` to React components that genuinely need interactivity.
    92→3. **Media via S3 + self-hosted CDN.** Images and videos are uploaded through the internal asset manager to S3 and served via `https://cdn.significa.co` (Cloudflare → Fly.io transform service → S3) with sharp-based image optimization. See `docs/04-MEDIA-ASSETS.md`.
    93→4. **Relationships via `reference()`.** Collections reference each other using Astro's `reference()` for build-time validation. Broken slugs break the build, not production.
    94→5. **Drafts via `.draft` filename suffix.** Name a file `my-post.draft.mdx` to mark it as a draft. The shared `contentLoader()` excludes `*.draft.*` files in production builds at the glob level. In development, drafts are included for preview. Publishing is a file rename — no frontmatter field needed.
    95→6. **Keep it simple.** If you're reaching for a library, stop and check if plain HTML/CSS or an Astro component can do it.
    96→7. **Fail at build time.** The site is managed by marketing and non-technical people. Every error caught at build time is one less bug in production.
    97→8. **Centralized MDX registration.** All MDX components are registered in `src/components/mdx/components.ts`. Add new components there — all slug pages pick them up automatically.
    98→
    99→## Build & Deploy
   100→
   101→```bash
   102→pnpm dev          # Development server with HMR
   103→pnpm build        # Build static site to dist/
   104→pnpm preview      # Preview built site locally
   105→pnpm check        # TypeScript + content validation (astro check)
   106→pnpm lint         # Full lint pipeline: Prettier + type check + ESLint
   107→pnpm lint:check   # ESLint only
   108→pnpm lint:fix     # ESLint auto-fix
   109→pnpm format       # Prettier auto-format src/
   110→pnpm format:check # Prettier check (no write)
   111→```
   112→
   113→### CI Pipeline
   114→
   115→```bash
   116→pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
   117→pnpm lint       # Prettier + type check + ESLint (catches all code quality issues)
   118→pnpm build      # Full build (catches MDX errors, broken references)
   119→```
   120→
   121→### Deploy
   122→
   123→Cloudflare Pages: push to main triggers build and deploy of `dist/`. Branch pushes create preview deployments.
   124→
   125→### Pre-Push Checklist
   126→
   127→Before pushing, run `pnpm lint && pnpm build` locally. This runs Prettier, type checking, and ESLint before building. Content errors caught locally are 10x faster than waiting for CI.
   128→
   129→## Reference Documentation
   130→
   131→- **Astro docs:** https://docs.astro.build
   132→- **Content Collections:** https://docs.astro.build/en/guides/content-collections/
   133→- **MDX in Astro:** https://docs.astro.build/en/guides/markdown-content/
   134→- **View Transitions:** https://docs.astro.build/en/guides/view-transitions/
   135→- **Islands Architecture:** https://docs.astro.build/en/concepts/islands/
   136→
   137→**Do not hallucinate APIs.** If you're unsure whether a method or config option exists, check the docs. Astro's API surface is small.

1→# Content Schema & Relationships
     2→
     3→Defines the "database": all content types, their fields, and how they relate to each other.
     4→
     5→## Schema Definition
     6→
     7→File: `src/content.config.ts` (at the root of `src/`, not inside `src/content/`)
     8→
     9→### Shared Schemas
    10→
    11→```ts
    12→import { defineCollection, reference, z } from "astro:content";
    13→import { glob, type Loader } from "astro/loaders";
    14→
    15→const seoSchema = z.object({
    16→  metaTitle: z.string().optional(),
    17→  metaDescription: z.string().optional(),
    18→  ogImage: z.string().optional(),
    19→});
    20→
    21→const metricSchema = z.object({
    22→  value: z.string(),
    23→  label: z.string(),
    24→});
    25→```
    26→
    27→### Content Loader
    28→
    29→All collections use a shared `contentLoader()` helper that centralizes glob patterns, ID generation, and **draft filtering**:
    30→
    31→```ts
    32→const contentLoader = ({ base, extensions }: { extensions: string[]; base: string }): Loader => {
    33→  const stripExtension = ({ entry }: { entry: string }) => {
    34→    return entry.replace(/\.(mdx?|yaml)$/, "");
    35→  };
    36→
    37→  // In development: include all files (drafts visible for preview)
    38→  // In production: exclude *.draft.* files at the glob level
    39→  const draftFilter = import.meta.env.MODE === "development" ? "**" : "!(*.draft)";
    40→  const extensionFilter = extensions.length > 1 ? `{${extensions.join(",")}}` : extensions[0];
    41→
    42→  return glob({
    43→    pattern: `**/${draftFilter}.${extensionFilter}`,
    44→    base,
    45→    generateId: stripExtension,
    46→  });
    47→};
    48→```
    49→
    50→This means:
    51→
    52→- **Development** (`pnpm dev`): all files are loaded, including `*.draft.*` files, so authors can preview drafts locally.
    53→- **Production** (`pnpm build`): files matching `*.draft.*` are excluded at the glob level — they never enter the content layer.
    54→- **Publishing a draft** is a file rename: `my-post.draft.mdx` → `my-post.mdx`.
    55→- **ID generation** strips file extensions for clean URL slugs: `example-project.mdx` → `example-project`.
    56→
    57→## Collections
    58→
    59→### Projects (MDX)
    60→
    61→Case studies. The richest content type.
    62→
    63→```ts
    64→const projects = defineCollection({
    65→  loader: contentLoader({
    66→    base: "src/content/projects",
    67→    extensions: ["mdx"],
    68→  }),
    69→  schema: z.object({
    70→    title: z.string(),
    71→    tagline: z.string().optional(),
    72→    client: z.string().optional(),
    73→    date: z.coerce.date(),
    74→    thumbnail: z.string(),
    75→    heroImage: z.string().optional(),
    76→    tags: z.array(z.string()).default([]),
    77→    metrics: z.array(metricSchema).default([]),
    78→    seo: seoSchema.optional(),
    79→    relatedProjects: z.array(reference("projects")).default([]),
    80→  }),
    81→});
    82→```
    83→
    84→| Field | Type | Required | Description |
    85→|---|---|---|---|
    86→| `title` | string | yes | Project name |
    87→| `tagline` | string | no | Short description for cards |
    88→| `client` | string | no | Client company name |
    89→| `date` | date | yes | Project date (used for sorting) |
    90→| `thumbnail` | string | yes | CDN path for listing cards |
    91→| `heroImage` | string | no | CDN path for showcase/detail header |
    92→| `tags` | string[] | no | Categorization tags |
    93→| `metrics` | metric[] | no | Key metrics shown on project cards (e.g. `{ value: "55%↑", label: "Conversion" }`) |
    94→| `seo` | object | no | Override meta title, description, OG image |
    95→| `relatedProjects` | reference[] | no | Build-time validated slugs of related projects |
    96→
    97→### Blog (MD/MDX)
    98→
    99→Blog posts. Supports both `.md` (no components) and `.mdx` (with components).
   100→
   101→```ts
   102→const blog = defineCollection({
   103→  loader: contentLoader({
   104→    extensions: ["md", "mdx"],
   105→    base: "src/content/blog",
   106→  }),
   107→  schema: z.object({
   108→    title: z.string(),
   109→    description: z.string().optional(),
   110→    date: z.coerce.date(),
   111→    author: z.string().optional(),
   112→    thumbnail: z.string().optional(),
   113→    tags: z.array(z.string()).default([]),
   114→    seo: seoSchema.optional(),
   115→    relatedProjects: z.array(reference("projects")).default([]),
   116→  }),
   117→});
   118→```
   119→
   120→| Field | Type | Required | Description |
   121→|---|---|---|---|
   122→| `title` | string | yes | Post title |
   123→| `description` | string | no | Short description for SEO and RSS. Falls back to title-based string if missing |
   124→| `date` | date | yes | Publication date |
   125→| `author` | string | no | Author name |
   126→| `thumbnail` | string | no | CDN path for listing cards |
   127→| `tags` | string[] | no | Categorization tags |
   128→| `seo` | object | no | Override meta title, description, OG image |
   129→| `relatedProjects` | reference[] | no | Cross-sell to project case studies |
   130→
   131→### Labs (MD/MDX)
   132→
   133→Open source projects and experiments.
   134→
   135→```ts
   136→const labs = defineCollection({
   137→  loader: contentLoader({
   138→    extensions: ["md", "mdx"],
   139→    base: "src/content/labs",
   140→  }),
   141→  schema: z.object({
   142→    title: z.string(),
   143→    description: z.string(),
   144→    date: z.coerce.date(),
   145→    repoUrl: z.string().url().optional(),
   146→    thumbnail: z.string().optional(),
   147→    tags: z.array(z.string()).default([]),
   148→    seo: seoSchema.optional(),
   149→  }),
   150→});
   151→```
   152→
   153→### Pages (MDX)
   154→
   155→Miscellaneous content pages (about, services, etc.) rendered by the catch-all `[...slug].astro`.
   156→
   157→```ts
   158→const pages = defineCollection({
   159→  loader: contentLoader({
   160→    extensions: ["mdx"],
   161→    base: "src/content/pages",
   162→  }),
   163→  schema: z.object({
   164→    title: z.string(),
   165→    seo: seoSchema.optional(),
   166→  }),
   167→});
   168→```
   169→
   170→### Highlights (YAML)
   171→
   172→Homepage curation. Each entry references an item in another collection using a discriminated union. Astro validates every slug at build time.
   173→
   174→```ts
   175→const highlights = defineCollection({
   176→  loader: contentLoader({
   177→    extensions: ["yaml"],
   178→    base: "src/content/highlights",
   179→  }),
   180→  schema: z.object({
   181→    label: z.string().optional(),
   182→    source: z.discriminatedUnion("collection", [
   183→      z.object({ collection: z.literal("projects"), entry: reference("projects") }),
   184→      z.object({ collection: z.literal("blog"), entry: reference("blog") }),
   185→      z.object({ collection: z.literal("labs"), entry: reference("labs") }),
   186→    ]),
   187→    order: z.number(),
   188→  }),
   189→});
   190→```
   191→
   192→Example YAML:
   193→
   194→```yaml
   195→# src/content/highlights/01-cool-project.yaml
   196→order: 1
   197→label: Featured Project
   198→source:
   199→  collection: projects
   200→  entry: example-project
   201→```
   202→
   203→If `example-project` doesn't exist in `src/content/projects/`, **the build fails** with a clear error. If the referenced file is a draft (e.g. `example-project.draft.mdx`), it works in dev but **fails the production build** — preventing links to unpublished content.
   204→
   205→### Clients (YAML)
   206→
   207→Client logo strip and references across the site.
   208→
   209→```ts
   210→const clients = defineCollection({
   211→  loader: contentLoader({
   212→    extensions: ["yaml"],
   213→    base: "src/content/clients",
   214→  }),
   215→  schema: z.object({
   216→    name: z.string(),
   217→    logo: z.string(),
   218→    url: z.string().url().optional(),
   219→    order: z.number().default(0),
   220→  }),
   221→});
   222→```
   223→
   224→| Field | Type | Required | Description |
   225→|---|---|---|---|
   226→| `name` | string | yes | Client company name |
   227→| `logo` | string | yes | Path to SVG logo in `public/` (e.g. `/logos/boeing.svg`) |
   228→| `url` | string | no | Client website URL |
   229→| `order` | number | no | Sort order (ascending). Default `0` |
   230→
   231→### Testimonials (YAML)
   232→
   233→Customer quotes for social proof sections.
   234→
   235→```ts
   236→const testimonials = defineCollection({
   237→  loader: contentLoader({
   238→    extensions: ["yaml"],
   239→    base: "src/content/testimonials",
   240→  }),
   241→  schema: z.object({
   242→    quote: z.string(),
   243→    author: z.string(),
   244→    role: z.string(),
   245→    company: z.string(),
   246→    avatar: z.string().optional(),
   247→    order: z.number().default(0),
   248→  }),
   249→});
   250→```
   251→
   252→| Field | Type | Required | Description |
   253→|---|---|---|---|
   254→| `quote` | string | yes | The testimonial text |
   255→| `author` | string | yes | Person's name |
   256→| `role` | string | yes | Job title |
   257→| `company` | string | yes | Company name |
   258→| `avatar` | string | no | CDN path to author photo |
   259→| `order` | number | no | Sort order (ascending). Default `0` |
   260→
   261→### Awards (YAML)
   262→
   263→Recognition and validation. Each award references a project.
   264→
   265→```ts
   266→const awards = defineCollection({
   267→  loader: contentLoader({
   268→    extensions: ["yaml"],
   269→    base: "src/content/awards",
   270→  }),
   271→  schema: z.object({
   272→    award: z.string(),
   273→    year: z.number(),
   274→    project: reference("projects"),
   275→    url: z.string().url().optional(),
   276→    order: z.number().default(0),
   277→  }),
   278→});
   279→```
   280→
   281→| Field | Type | Required | Description |
   282→|---|---|---|---|
   283→| `award` | string | yes | Award name (e.g. "Red Dot", "Awwwards SOTD") |
   284→| `year` | number | yes | Year awarded |
   285→| `project` | reference | yes | Project that won — build-time validated |
   286→| `url` | string | no | External link to award page |
   287→| `order` | number | no | Sort order (ascending). Default `0` |
   288→
   289→### Handbook Groups (YAML)
   290→
   291→Visual categories shown on the handbook index page. Each handbook page references one group.
   292→
   293→```ts
   294→const handbookGroups = defineCollection({
   295→  loader: contentLoader({
   296→    extensions: ["yaml"],
   297→    base: "src/content/handbook-groups",
   298→  }),
   299→  schema: z.object({
   300→    title: z.string(),
   301→    order: z.number(),
   302→  }),
   303→});
   304→```
   305→
   306→| Field | Type | Required | Description |
   307→|---|---|---|---|
   308→| `title` | string | yes | Display name shown on the index page |
   309→| `order` | number | yes | Sort order of the group on the index page |
   310→
   311→Filenames act as slugs referenced by handbook pages (e.g. `engineering.yaml` → `group: engineering`).
   312→
   313→### Handbook (MDX)
   314→
   315→Internal company documentation. File path determines both the URL and the parent-child hierarchy.
   316→
   317→```ts
   318→const handbook = defineCollection({
   319→  loader: contentLoader({
   320→    extensions: ["mdx"],
   321→    base: "src/content/handbook",
   322→  }),
   323→  schema: z.object({
   324→    title: z.string(),
   325→    description: z.string().optional(),
   326→    date: z.coerce.date(),
   327→    group: reference("handbook-groups"),
   328→    order: z.number().default(0),
   329→    seo: seoSchema.optional(),
   330→  }),
   331→});
   332→```
   333→
   334→| Field | Type | Required | Description |
   335→|---|---|---|---|
   336→| `title` | string | yes | Page title |
   337→| `description` | string | no | Short description for SEO and child page listings |
   338→| `date` | date | yes | Last updated date |
   339→| `group` | reference | yes | Which group this page belongs to — build-time validated |
   340→| `order` | number | no | Sort order within the group on the index. Default `0` |
   341→| `seo` | object | no | Override meta title, description, OG image |
   342→
   343→**File structure determines hierarchy:**
   344→
   345→```
   346→src/content/handbook/
   347→├── culture.mdx                              → /handbook/culture          (top-level)
   348→├── how-we-build-software.mdx               → /handbook/how-we-build-software  (top-level, has children)
   349→└── how-we-build-software/
   350→    ├── front-end-development.mdx           → /handbook/how-we-build-software/front-end-development
   351→    └── back-end-development.mdx            → /handbook/how-we-build-software/back-end-development
   352→```
   353→
   354→- A page becomes a **parent** when a sibling directory shares its name and contains MDX files.
   355→- Children inherit their parent's URL as a prefix.
   356→- Nesting beyond one level is supported but should be used sparingly.
   357→- The `group` field is still required on child pages — it controls which group they appear under in sidebar navigation.
   358→
   359→## Export
   360→
   361→All collections must be exported from `src/content.config.ts`:
   362→
   363→```ts
   364→export const collections = {
   365→  projects,
   366→  blog,
   367→  labs,
   368→  pages,
   369→  highlights,
   370→  clients,
   371→  testimonials,
   372→  awards,
   373→  "handbook-groups": handbookGroups,
   374→  handbook,
   375→};
   376→```
   377→
   378→## Draft System
   379→
   380→Drafts are managed via the **`.draft` filename suffix**, not a frontmatter field.
   381→
   382→### How It Works
   383→
   384→| File | Dev (`pnpm dev`) | Prod (`pnpm build`) |
   385→|---|---|---|
   386→| `cool-project.mdx` | ✅ Loaded | ✅ Loaded |
   387→| `cool-project.draft.mdx` | ✅ Loaded (preview) | ❌ Excluded at glob level |
   388→
   389→### Workflow
   390→
   391→1. **Create a draft:** name it `my-post.draft.mdx` (or `.draft.md`, `.draft.yaml`)
   392→2. **Preview locally:** run `pnpm dev` — drafts are included in the content layer
   393→3. **Publish:** rename `my-post.draft.mdx` → `my-post.mdx`
   394→4. **Unpublish:** rename `my-post.mdx` → `my-post.draft.mdx`
   395→
   396→### Important: References to Drafts
   397→
   398→If a non-draft entry (e.g. a highlight or award) references a draft entry:
   399→- **In dev:** works fine — the draft is in the collection
   400→- **In prod build:** fails with a build error — the draft is excluded, so the reference is broken
   401→
   402→This is intentional. It prevents shipping pages that link to unpublished content.
   403→
   404→## How Relationships Work
   405→
   406→All cross-content references use `reference()` from Astro for compile-time validation. `reference()` returns `{ id, collection }` objects, **not** full entries. You must resolve them with `getEntry()`.
   407→
   408→### Pattern 1: Related Projects in a Case Study
   409→
   410→Frontmatter in `src/content/projects/cool-project.mdx`:
   411→
   412→```yaml
   413→---
   414→title: Cool Project
   415→client: Acme Corp
   416→date: 2024-06-01
   417→thumbnail: /images/projects/cool-project/thumb.jpg
   418→relatedProjects:
   419→  - another-project
   420→  - third-project
   421→---
   422→```
   423→
   424→If `another-project` or `third-project` don't exist as files in `src/content/projects/`, **the build fails immediately**.
   425→
   426→In the page template `src/pages/projects/[slug].astro`:
   427→
   428→```astro
   429→---
   430→import { getCollection, getEntry, render } from "astro:content";
   431→import { mdxComponents } from "../../components/mdx/components";
   432→
   433→export async function getStaticPaths() {
   434→  const projects = await getCollection("projects");
   435→  return projects.map((project) => ({
   436→    params: { slug: project.id },
   437→    props: { project },
   438→  }));
   439→}
   440→
   441→const { project } = Astro.props;
   442→const { Content } = await render(project);
   443→
   444→// Resolve related projects
   445→const relatedProjects = project.data.relatedProjects?.length
   446→  ? await Promise.all(project.data.relatedProjects.map((ref) => getEntry(ref)))
   447→  : [];
   448→---
   449→
   450→<Content components={mdxComponents} />
   451→```
   452→
   453→### Pattern 2: Homepage Highlights (Cross-Collection)
   454→
   455→Each highlight references an entry in another collection via `discriminatedUnion`. Resolved in `src/lib/collections.ts`:
   456→
   457→```ts
   458→export async function getResolvedHighlights() {
   459→  const allHighlights = await getCollection("highlights");
   460→  const sorted = allHighlights.sort((a, b) => a.data.order - b.data.order);
   461→
   462→  const resolved = await Promise.all(
   463→    sorted.map(async (h) => {
   464→      const entry = await getEntry(
   465→        h.data.source.collection,
   466→        h.data.source.entry.id,
   467→      );
   468→      return {
   469→        label: h.data.label,
   470→        collection: h.data.source.collection,
   471→        entry,
   472→      };
   473→    }),
   474→  );
   475→
   476→  return resolved;
   477→}
   478→```
   479→
   480→### Pattern 3: Awards with Project References
   481→
   482→Awards reference projects, resolved in `src/lib/collections.ts`:
   483→
   484→```ts
   485→export async function getAwards() {
   486→  const allAwards = await getCollection("awards");
   487→  const sorted = allAwards.sort((a, b) => a.data.order - b.data.order);
   488→
   489→  const resolved = await Promise.all(
   490→    sorted.map(async (a) => {
   491→      const project = await getEntry("projects", a.data.project.id);
   492→      return {
   493→        award: a.data.award,
   494→        year: a.data.year,
   495→        url: a.data.url,
   496→        project,
   497→      };
   498→    }),
   499→  );
   500→
   501→  return resolved;
   502→}
   503→```
   504→
   505→### Pattern 4: MDX Cross-Sell Component
   506→
   507→Inside any `.mdx` file:
   508→
   509→```mdx
   510→<ProjectCrossSell slug="another-project" />
   511→```
   512→
   513→The component resolves the slug with `getEntry()` and throws at build time if not found.
   514→
   515→## Collection Helper Functions
   516→
   517→All query helpers live in `src/lib/collections.ts`. These handle sorting and reference resolution. **No draft filtering is needed** — drafts are already excluded by the content loader in production.
   518→
   519→| Function | Returns |
   520→|---|---|
   521→| `getProjects()` | All projects, sorted by date desc |
   522→| `getPosts()` | All blog posts, sorted by date desc |
   523→| `getLabs()` | All labs, sorted by date desc |
   524→| `getClients()` | All clients, sorted by order asc |
   525→| `getTestimonials()` | All testimonials, sorted by order asc |
   526→| `getResolvedHighlights()` | Highlights with referenced entries resolved |
   527→| `getAwards()` | Awards with referenced projects resolved |
   528→| `getHandbookGroups()` | All handbook groups, sorted by order asc |
   529→| `getHandbookPages()` | All handbook pages, sorted by order asc |
   530→| `getTopLevelHandbookPages()` | Handbook pages without a parent |
   531→| `getHandbookChildren(parentId)` | Direct children of a handbook page |
   532→| `getHandbookParent(entry)` | Parent entry for a handbook page, or null |
   533→| `getHandbookBreadcrumbs(entry)` | Ancestor chain from root to the current page |
   534→| `getHandbookIndex()` | Full grouped structure for the index page |
   535→
   536→## Important Notes
   537→
   538→- **`reference()` returns `{ id, collection }` objects**, not the full entry. Resolve with `getEntry(ref)`.
   539→- **Handbook parent-child hierarchy is file-path derived.** A page at `how-we-build-software/front-end.mdx` is automatically a child of `how-we-build-software.mdx`. No `parent` field in frontmatter — the relationship is implicit in the directory structure.
   540→- **Handbook groups use `reference("handbook-groups")`**, so a typo in `group:` breaks the build, not production.
   541→- **Drafts use the `.draft` filename suffix.** No frontmatter field, no query-time filtering. The content loader handles it automatically.
   542→- **Blog supports both `.md` and `.mdx`.** Blog posts that don't need custom components can use plain `.md`.
   543→- **YAML for structured data without prose.** Highlights, clients, testimonials, awards.
   544→- **Config file location:** `src/content.config.ts` (at root of `src/`, both this path and `src/content/content.config.ts` work in Astro v5+, but we use the root path).
   545→- **MDX components are centralized:** registered once in `src/components/mdx/components.ts`, used by all slug pages.

1→# MDX Components
     2→
     3→Custom components used inside `.mdx` content files. These are **Astro components** (not React): they run at build time with zero client JS.
     4→
     5→## Registering Components for MDX
     6→
     7→All MDX components are registered in a single file: `src/components/mdx/components.ts`. Every `[slug].astro` page imports from there — no duplication.
     8→
     9→```ts
    10→// src/components/mdx/components.ts
    11→import MediaImage from "./media-image.astro";
    12→import MediaVideo from "./media-video.astro";
    13→import ComparisonBlock from "./comparison-block.astro";
    14→import Metrics from "./metrics.astro";
    15→import ProjectCrossSell from "./project-cross-sell.astro";
    16→
    17→export const mdxComponents = {
    18→  MediaImage,
    19→  MediaVideo,
    20→  ComparisonBlock,
    21→  Metrics,
    22→  ProjectCrossSell,
    23→};
    24→```
    25→
    26→In your `[slug].astro` page, import and pass:
    27→
    28→```astro
    29→---
    30→import { mdxComponents } from "../../components/mdx/components";
    31→
    32→const { Content } = await render(entry);
    33→---
    34→
    35→<Content components={mdxComponents} />
    36→```
    37→
    38→## Component Catalog
    39→
    40→### MediaImage
    41→
    42→Renders an optimized image via the CDN with responsive `srcset`. Always use this instead of raw `![alt](url)` for captions, sizing control, and automatic image optimization.
    43→
    44→The `src` prop takes the asset path (as returned by the asset manager). The component prepends the CDN hostname (`https://cdn.significa.co`) and appends optimization query parameters automatically.
    45→
    46→```mdx
    47→<MediaImage
    48→  src="/projects/cool-project/hero.jpg"
    49→  alt="Dashboard overview"
    50→  width={1200}
    51→  height={630}
    52→  caption="The main dashboard after redesign"
    53→/>
    54→```
    55→
    56→**Required props:** `src`, `alt`, `width`, `height`
    57→**Optional:** `caption`, `eager` (for above-the-fold images), `sizes` (responsive sizes attr), `quality` (0-100, default 80)
    58→
    59→### MediaVideo
    60→
    61→Embeds a video served via the CDN. The `poster` image goes through the same transform pipeline as `MediaImage`.
    62→
    63→```mdx
    64→<MediaVideo
    65→  src="/projects/cool-project/demo.mp4"
    66→  poster="/projects/cool-project/demo-poster.jpg"
    67→  caption="Prototype walkthrough"
    68→/>
    69→```
    70→
    71→### ComparisonBlock
    72→
    73→Side-by-side before/after or A/B comparison.
    74→
    75→```mdx
    76→<ComparisonBlock
    77→  before={{
    78→    src: "/images/projects/x/before.jpg",
    79→    label: "Before redesign"
    80→  }}
    81→  after={{
    82→    src: "/images/projects/x/after.jpg",
    83→    label: "After redesign"
    84→  }}
    85→/>
    86→```
    87→
    88→### Metrics
    89→
    90→Key numbers/stats display.
    91→
    92→```mdx
    93→<Metrics
    94→  items={[
    95→    { value: "3x", label: "Conversion rate increase" },
    96→    { value: "40%", label: "Faster load time" },
    97→    { value: "98", label: "Lighthouse score" },
    98→  ]}
    99→/>
   100→```
   101→
   102→### ProjectCrossSell
   103→
   104→Card linking to another project. See `02-CONTENT-SCHEMA.md` for the implementation pattern.
   105→
   106→```mdx
   107→<ProjectCrossSell slug="another-project" />
   108→```
   109→
   110→## Component Growth Strategy
   111→
   112→We start with 5 components. New components are added when content demands them, not eagerly. When a content author needs a pattern that doesn't exist, that's the signal to build it.
   113→
   114→**Components to consider adding (by priority):**
   115→
   116→| Component | Justification | Priority |
   117→|---|---|---|
   118→| `ImageGrid` | Show multiple screens side by side (2-4 columns) | Must-have |
   119→| `Section` | Background color changes, full-bleed, visual chapters | Must-have |
   120→| `Testimonial` | Client voice: quote, name, role, company | Must-have |
   121→| `CTA` | Conversion points within content | Must-have |
   122→| `PullQuote` / `Callout` | Break monotony, highlight insights | Should-have |
   123→| `DeviceFrame` | Contextualize screenshots (phone/desktop) | Should-have |
   124→
   125→## Adding New MDX Components
   126→
   127→1. Create the `.astro` file in `src/components/mdx/`
   128→2. Define `interface Props` with TypeScript
   129→3. Import and add to the `mdxComponents` map in `src/components/mdx/components.ts`
   130→4. Add usage example to this documentation
   131→5. Test with `pnpm build` to verify
   132→
   133→All slug pages import from `components.ts`, so step 3 is the only wiring needed.
   134→
   135→## Rules for MDX Components
   136→
   137→1. **Astro components only.** No React unless the component needs client interactivity.
   138→2. **Throw on bad data.** Missing props or unresolved slugs must throw with a clear error message at build time.
   139→3. **No data fetching.** All data comes from props or content collections.
   140→4. **Keep them simple.** Render what they receive. Business logic stays in page templates.
   141→5. **Document the props interface.** Every component needs a clear `interface Props`.
   142→

1→# Media & Assets 2→ 3→## Architecture 4→ 5→Media files are managed through Significa's internal asset manager (`assets.significa.co`), which uploads files to **S3**. A self-hosted image transform service on **Fly.io** handles on-the-fly image optimization via **sharp**, fronted by **Cloudflare** for edge caching. 6→ 7→- **Asset Manager** (`assets.significa.co`): uploads images/videos to S3, returns URLs 8→- **CDN** (`cdn.significa.co`): Cloudflare edge cache → Fly.io transform service → S3 9→- **Image transforms**: sharp-based, triggered via URL query parameters 10→ 11→Content authors reference media by their S3 path. The `MediaImage` component constructs optimized CDN URLs with transform parameters automatically. 12→ 13→``` 14→Author uploads via asset manager 15→ -> File stored in S3 16→ -> Referenced in MDX as path (e.g. /projects/cool-project/hero.jpg) 17→ -> MediaImage builds CDN URL with transforms 18→ -> Cloudflare edge serves cached version (or Fly transforms on cache miss) 19→``` 20→ 21→## CDN Hostname 22→ 23→``` 24→https://cdn.significa.co 25→``` 26→ 27→All media URLs are constructed by prepending this hostname to the asset path. 28→ 29→## Image Transform Service 30→ 31→The transform service runs on Fly.io (`assets.significa.co`) with sharp. It transforms images on-the-fly via query parameters. Transformed images are cached in two layers: S3 (persistent, survives deploys) and Cloudflare edge (global, automatic). 32→ 33→### URL Pattern 34→ 35→``` 36→https://cdn.significa.co/?width=800&quality=80&format=webp 37→``` 38→ 39→### Available Parameters 40→ 41→| Parameter | Values | Default | Description | 42→|---|---|---|---| 43→| `width` | integer (px) | original | Resize to width, maintain aspect ratio | 44→| `height` | integer (px) | original | Resize to height, maintain aspect ratio | 45→| `quality` | 0-100 | 80 | Compression level | 46→| `format` | webp, avif, jpeg, png | auto-negotiated | Force output format | 47→ 48→When no `format` is specified, the service auto-negotiates from the `Accept` header: AVIF → WebP → original format. This gives every browser the best format it supports without clients needing to know. 49→ 50→### Caching 51→ 52→**Layer 1 — S3 transform cache (persistent):** Transformed images are stored back to S3 under a `_transforms/` prefix, keyed by a hash of (original path + params). Sharp runs exactly once per unique combination. 53→ 54→**Layer 2 — Cloudflare edge cache (global):** Responses include `Cache-Control: public, max-age=31536000, immutable`. After the first request, Cloudflare serves directly from the edge — never hits Fly again until eviction. 55→ 56→### Common Transform Recipes 57→ 58→``` 59→# Thumbnail (400px wide, optimized) 60→?width=400&quality=80 61→ 62→# Hero image (full width, WebP) 63→?width=1920&quality=85&format=webp 64→ 65→# Square avatar (use CSS object-fit for cropping) 66→?width=200&quality=80 67→``` 68→ 69→## Referencing Media in Content 70→ 71→### In frontmatter 72→ 73→```yaml 74→thumbnail: /projects/cool-project/thumb.jpg 75→``` 76→ 77→### In MDX body 78→ 79→```mdx 80→ 87→``` 88→ 89→The `MediaImage` component prepends the CDN hostname and appends optimization parameters automatically. Content authors only need to provide the asset path as returned by the asset manager. 90→ 91→## MediaImage Component Behavior 92→ 93→`MediaImage` constructs responsive image URLs using the transform service: 94→ 95→1. Takes the `src` path (e.g. `/projects/cool-project/hero.jpg`) 96→2. Prepends the CDN hostname: `https://cdn.significa.co/projects/cool-project/hero.jpg` 97→3. Generates `srcset` with multiple widths for responsive loading 98→4. Appends `quality` and lets the service auto-negotiate format (WebP/AVIF when supported) 99→ 100→Example output (without caption): 101→ 102→```html 103→ 117→``` 118→ 119→When a `caption` is provided, the output wraps in `

1→# Rules & Anti-Patterns 2→ 3→## DO 4→ 5→- **Use Astro components by default.** Only reach for React when you need client-side interactivity (e.g. mobile menu toggle, form validation). Even then, prefer `client:visible` over `client:load`. 6→- **Use semantic HTML.** `
`, `
`, `
`, `
`, `
`, `
`, ``. Not everything is a `
`. 7→- **Fail at build time.** If a slug reference is broken, a required field is missing, or data is invalid: throw. The build breaks, not production. 8→- **Keep pages thin.** Pages (`src/pages/`) should fetch data and delegate rendering to layout/components. No complex logic in pages. 9→- **Use TypeScript everywhere.** Type your props with `interface Props`, type your data, type your utils. No `any`. 10→- **Use `reference()` for cross-collection links.** Build-time validated. Broken slugs break the build, not production. 11→- **Use `pnpm`.** Not npm, not yarn. 12→- **Use design tokens for colors and typography.** Colors and font sizes come from `tokens.css` custom properties. No hex colors, no arbitrary font sizes. Spacing uses raw values (4px grid guideline, `rem` for vertical, `px` for horizontal when appropriate). See `docs/08-DESIGN-TOKENS.md`. 13→- **Use `MediaImage` for all images in content.** Never raw `` tags. It constructs CDN URLs with responsive `srcset` and optimization parameters automatically. 14→- **Drafts use the `.draft` filename suffix.** Name a file `my-post.draft.mdx` to mark it as a draft. In production builds, `.draft` files are excluded at the glob level — they never enter the content layer. In development (`pnpm dev`), drafts are included so authors can preview them locally. Publishing is a file rename: `my-post.draft.mdx` → `my-post.mdx`. No frontmatter field needed. 15→- **Use kebab-case for all filenames.** Components, pages, utilities, styles, content — everything is kebab-case (`media-image.astro`, `content-errors.ts`, `global.css`). Enforced by ESLint via `eslint-plugin-check-file`. 16→ 17→## DO NOT 18→ 19→- **Do not use `getStaticPaths` for non-dynamic routes.** Only `[slug].astro` files need it. Static pages like `index.astro` call `getCollection()` directly. 20→- **Do not add a CSS framework.** Scoped `

1→# Astro Reference 2→ 3→> Deep reference for building production static sites with Astro. 4→> Based on Astro v5 (latest stable). Astro 6 is in beta — do not upgrade until stable. 5→> For project-specific patterns, see `02-CONTENT-SCHEMA.md` and `03-MDX-COMPONENTS.md`. 6→ 7→--- 8→ 9→## Core Mental Model 10→ 11→Astro is a **content-first static site generator** that ships **zero JavaScript by default**. Every `.astro` component renders to HTML at build time. JavaScript only reaches the browser when you explicitly opt in via `client:*` directives on framework components (React, Vue, Svelte). 12→ 13→This is the **island architecture**: a sea of static HTML with interactive islands of JavaScript where needed. 14→ 15→``` 16→Static HTML (default, no JS) 17→ └── Interactive Island (React/Vue/Svelte, explicit opt-in) 18→ └── Hydrated only when triggered (load, idle, visible, media) 19→``` 20→ 21→--- 22→ 23→## Astro Component Anatomy 24→ 25→Two-part structure: server frontmatter + HTML template. 26→ 27→```astro 28→--- 29→// Server-side only: runs at build time, never shipped to client 30→import { getCollection } from 'astro:content'; 31→import Card from '../components/Card.astro'; 32→ 33→interface Props { 34→ title: string; 35→ count?: number; 36→} 37→ 38→const { title, count = 0 } = Astro.props; 39→const posts = await getCollection('blog'); 40→--- 41→ 42→ 43→
44→

    • 48→ ))} 49→

51→ 52→ 57→``` 58→ 59→### Key Rules 60→- Frontmatter is TypeScript, runs on server only 61→- Template uses JSX-like syntax but outputs HTML 62→- `Astro.props` for component props (type with `interface Props`) 63→- `Astro.url`, `Astro.request`, `Astro.redirect()` available in pages 64→- Styles are scoped by default; use `is:global` for global styles 65→- `` for content projection (named slots: ``) 66→- All async operations in frontmatter are awaited before rendering 67→ 68→--- 69→ 70→## Content Collections (v5+) 71→ 72→The content system. Define schemas with Zod, load content with loaders, query with type-safe APIs. 73→ 74→### Schema Definition 75→ 76→File: `src/content.config.ts` (at root of `src/`; `src/content/content.config.ts` also works) 77→ 78→```typescript 79→import { defineCollection, reference, z } from 'astro:content'; 80→import { glob } from 'astro/loaders'; 81→ 82→const blog = defineCollection({ 83→ loader: contentLoader({ extensions: ['md', 'mdx'], base: 'src/content/blog' }), 84→ schema: z.object({ 85→ title: z.string(), 86→ date: z.coerce.date(), 87→ tags: z.array(z.string()).default([]), 88→ thumbnail: z.string().url().optional(), 89→ // Cross-collection reference: build fails if slug doesn't exist 90→ relatedPosts: z.array(reference('blog')).default([]), 91→ }), 92→}); 93→ 94→export const collections = { blog }; 95→``` 96→ 97→### Loaders 98→ 99→```typescript 100→// File-based (most common) 101→glob({ pattern: '**/*.mdx', base: 'src/content/blog' }) 102→ 103→// Single file 104→file('src/data/authors.json') 105→ 106→// Custom loader (API, database, anything) 107→{ 108→ loader: async ({ store }) => { 109→ const data = await fetch('https://api.example.com/posts'); 110→ const posts = await data.json(); 111→ for (const post of posts) { 112→ store.set({ id: post.slug, data: post }); 113→ } 114→ } 115→} 116→``` 117→ 118→### Querying Content 119→ 120→```typescript 121→import { getCollection, getEntry, render } from 'astro:content'; 122→ 123→// Get all entries (drafts already excluded in production by the loader) 124→const posts = await getCollection('blog'); 125→ 126→// Get single entry 127→const post = await getEntry('blog', 'my-post-slug'); 128→ 129→// Resolve a reference 130→const related = await getEntry(post.data.relatedPosts[0]); 131→ 132→// Render MDX/Markdown to component 133→const { Content } = await render(post); 134→``` 135→ 136→### References (Cross-Collection) 137→ 138→`reference()` creates build-time validated links between collections: 139→ 140→```typescript 141→// In schema 142→relatedProjects: z.array(reference('projects')).default([]) 143→ 144→// reference() returns { id, collection } objects, NOT full entries 145→// Must resolve with getEntry(): 146→const resolved = await Promise.all( 147→ entry.data.relatedProjects.map(ref => getEntry(ref)) 148→); 149→``` 150→ 151→### Discriminated Unions (Cross-Collection Polymorphism) 152→ 153→```typescript 154→const highlights = defineCollection({ 155→ loader: glob({ pattern: '**/*.yaml', base: 'src/content/highlights' }), 156→ schema: z.object({ 157→ order: z.number(), 158→ source: z.discriminatedUnion('collection', [ 159→ z.object({ collection: z.literal('projects'), entry: reference('projects') }), 160→ z.object({ collection: z.literal('blog'), entry: reference('blog') }), 161→ ]), 162→ }), 163→}); 164→``` 165→ 166→--- 167→ 168→## Dynamic Routes (Static Generation) 169→ 170→For `[param].astro` files, `getStaticPaths()` tells Astro which pages to generate: 171→ 172→```astro 173→--- 174→// src/pages/blog/[slug].astro 175→import { getCollection, render } from 'astro:content'; 176→import Layout from '../../layouts/Layout.astro'; 177→ 178→export async function getStaticPaths() { 179→ const posts = await getCollection('blog'); 180→ return posts.map(post => ({ 181→ params: { slug: post.id }, 182→ props: { post }, 183→ })); 184→} 185→ 186→const { post } = Astro.props; 187→const { Content } = await render(post); 188→--- 189→ 190→ 191→
192→

195→ 196→``` 197→ 198→### Catch-All Routes 199→ 200→```astro 201→// src/pages/[...slug].astro 202→// Matches /anything, /anything/nested, etc. 203→export async function getStaticPaths() { 204→ const pages = await getCollection('pages'); 205→ return pages.map(page => ({ 206→ params: { slug: page.id }, 207→ props: { page }, 208→ })); 209→} 210→``` 211→ 212→**Rule:** Only `[slug].astro` and `[...slug].astro` files need `getStaticPaths()`. Static pages like `index.astro` just call `getCollection()` directly. 213→ 214→--- 215→ 216→## MDX Integration 217→ 218→MDX = Markdown + JSX components. Content authors write markdown with embedded components. 219→ 220→### Setup 221→ 222→```javascript 223→// astro.config.mjs 224→import mdx from '@astrojs/mdx'; 225→ 226→export default defineConfig({ 227→ integrations: [mdx()], 228→}); 229→``` 230→ 231→### Passing Components to MDX 232→ 233→```astro 234→--- 235→import MediaImage from '../components/mdx/media-image.astro'; 236→import Metrics from '../components/mdx/metrics.astro'; 237→ 238→const { Content } = await render(post); 239→ 240→const mdxComponents = { 241→ MediaImage, 242→ Metrics, 243→ // Override default HTML elements: 244→ h1: CustomH1, 245→ img: CustomImage, 246→}; 247→--- 248→ 249→ 250→``` 251→ 252→### MDX Component Rules 253→1. Use **Astro components** for MDX components (not React): zero JS, build-time only 254→2. **Throw on bad data**: if a slug doesn't resolve or a prop is missing, throw at build time 255→3. **No fetching**: all data comes from props or content collections 256→4. **Keep them simple**: render what they receive, business logic stays in page templates 257→ 258→--- 259→ 260→## Island Architecture (Partial Hydration) 261→ 262→Only framework components (React, Vue, Svelte) can be islands. Astro components are always static. 263→ 264→### Client Directives 265→ 266→```astro 267→ 268→ 269→ 270→ 271→ 272→``` 273→ 274→### When to Use Islands 275→- Mobile navigation toggle 276→- Form validation with live feedback 277→- Interactive carousels or sliders 278→- Search with live results 279→- Theme toggle with persistence 280→- Anything requiring `useState`, `useEffect`, or event handlers beyond simple links 281→ 282→### When NOT to Use Islands 283→- Static content display 284→- Navigation links 285→- Image galleries (use CSS or `
`) 286→- Accordions (use `
