1→# Cover Images Feature - Implementation Summary
     2→
     3→## Overview
     4→
     5→I've successfully added support for optional cover images on all handbook pages. This feature allows you to add visual context and improve the presentation of handbook content.
     6→
     7→## What Changed
     8→
     9→### 1. Schema Update
    10→**File:** `src/content.config.ts`
    11→
    12→Added optional `coverImage` field to the handbook collection:
    13→```typescript
    14→coverImage: z.string().optional()
    15→```
    16→
    17→This field accepts full URLs (e.g., `https://cdn.significa.co/handbook/code-of-ethics-cover.jpg`).
    18→
    19→### 2. UI Implementation
    20→**File:** `src/pages/handbook/[...slug].astro`
    21→
    22→Enhanced the detail page to display cover images:
    23→- Imports CDN helpers (`cdnUrl`, `cdnSrcset`, `DEFAULT_QUALITY`)
    24→- Conditionally renders cover image when `coverImage` field is present
    25→- Displays between page header and content
    26→- Optimized for performance with eager loading and high fetchpriority
    27→
    28→### 3. Documentation Updates
    29→
    30→Updated multiple documentation files:
    31→- `docs/04-MEDIA-ASSETS.md` - Added handbook cover images section
    32→- `docs/10-PARENT-CHILD-PAGES.md` - Added cover image usage and guidelines
    33→- `docs/QUICK-PARENT-CHILD-GUIDE.md` - Added quick reference for cover images
    34→- `PARENT-CHILD-SETUP.md` - Updated with cover image information
    35→
    36→### 4. Example Implementation
    37→**File:** `src/content/handbook/mission-and-values/code-of-ethics.mdx`
    38→
    39→Added cover image to demonstrate the feature:
    40→```yaml
    41→coverImage: https://cdn.significa.co/handbook/code-of-ethics-cover.jpg
    42→```
    43→
    44→## How to Use
    45→
    46→### Adding a Cover Image
    47→
    48→Simply add the `coverImage` field to your handbook page frontmatter with a full URL:
    49→
    50→```yaml
    51→---
    52→title: Your Page Title
    53→description: Your description
    54→date: 2024-01-15
    55→coverImage: https://cdn.significa.co/handbook/your-image.jpg
    56→group: your-group
    57→order: 1
    58→---
    59→```
    60→
    61→### Image Guidelines
    62→
    63→**Dimensions:**
    64→- Recommended: 1200×630px or larger
    65→- Aspect ratio: 16:9 or 2:1 works best
    66→
    67→**Format:**
    68→- JPEG for photographs
    69→- PNG for graphics with transparency
    70→
    71→**URL Format:**
    72→- Use full URLs: `https://cdn.significa.co/handbook/image-name.jpg`
    73→- External URLs also supported: `https://images.unsplash.com/photo-xyz`
    74→- Any public image URL works
    75→
    76→**File Size:**
    77→- Optimize before uploading
    78→- CDN images will be automatically optimized further with quality settings
    79→- External URLs are used as-is (no optimization applied)
    80→
    81→## Display Behavior
    82→
    83→### Visual Presentation
    84→- Full-width within content area (max 840px on desktop)
    85→- Rounded corners (8px border-radius)
    86→- Positioned between page header and content
    87→- Overflow hidden for clean edges
    88→
    89→### Performance Optimization
    90→- **Eager loading:** `loading="eager"`
    91→- **High priority:** `fetchpriority="high"`
    92→- **Sync decoding:** `decoding="sync"`
    93→- **Responsive srcset:** Automatically generated for multiple screen sizes
    94→- **Optimized for LCP:** Cover images load immediately for better Core Web Vitals
    95→
    96→### Responsive Behavior
    97→```html
    98→sizes="(max-width: 768px) 100vw, 840px"
    99→```
   100→- Mobile: Full viewport width
   101→- Desktop: Constrained to content width (840px)
   102→
   103→## Technical Details
   104→
   105→### URL Handling
   106→
   107→The `cdnUrl()` helper function handles all URL formats:
   108→- **CDN URLs** (`https://cdn.significa.co/...`) → Adds optimization parameters
   109→- **External URLs** → Used as-is without modification
   110→- **Relative paths** (legacy) → Prepends CDN hostname
   111→
   112→### CDN Integration
   113→
   114→Cover images leverage the existing CDN infrastructure:
   115→- Served from `https://cdn.significa.co`
   116→- Automatic srcset generation via `cdnSrcset()` (CDN URLs only)
   117→- Quality optimization via `cdnUrl()` with width and quality parameters
   118→- Format auto-negotiation (WebP/AVIF based on browser support)
   119→- External URLs bypass srcset generation (used directly)
   120→
   121→### Build-Time Validation
   122→
   123→- Schema validates `coverImage` is a string (if provided)
   124→- Invalid paths don't break the build (handled gracefully at runtime)
   125→- Missing images won't cause build failures
   126→- Type-safe with TypeScript strict mode
   127→
   128→## Examples
   129→
   130→### With Cover Image
   131→```yaml
   132→---
   133→title: Code of Ethics
   134→description: Our ethical guidelines and principles.
   135→date: 2024-01-15
   136→coverImage: https://cdn.significa.co/handbook/code-of-ethics-cover.jpg
   137→group: knowing-significa
   138→order: 1
   139→---
   140→```
   141→
   142→### External URL Example
   143→```yaml
   144→---
   145→title: Design Principles
   146→description: How we approach design.
   147→date: 2024-01-20
   148→coverImage: https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1234567890
   149→group: design
   150→order: 1
   151→---
   152→```
   153→
   154→### Without Cover Image
   155→```yaml
   156→---
   157→title: Manifesto
   158→description: What we believe in.
   159→date: 2024-01-10
   160→group: knowing-significa
   161→order: 2
   162→---
   163→```
   164→
   165→Both work perfectly fine. The cover image is completely optional.
   166→
   167→## Testing
   168→
   169→**Validation:**
   170→- ✅ `pnpm check` - 0 errors, 0 warnings
   171→- ✅ `pnpm build` - 26 pages built successfully
   172→- ✅ Types generated correctly
   173→- ✅ Cover image displays on Code of Ethics page
   174→
   175→**Test URLs:**
   176→- Index: http://localhost:4322/handbook
   177→- With cover: http://localhost:4322/handbook/mission-and-values/code-of-ethics
   178→- Without cover: http://localhost:4322/handbook/manifesto
   179→
   180→## Usage Recommendations
   181→
   182→### When to Use Cover Images
   183→
   184→✅ **Good use cases:**
   185→- Visual context for the content (e.g., team photo for culture page)
   186→- Setting the tone (e.g., ethics imagery for Code of Ethics)
   187→- Breaking up long handbook sections
   188→- Creating visual distinction between major sections
   189→
   190→❌ **Avoid:**
   191→- Adding images just because you can
   192→- Using low-quality or irrelevant images
   193→- Heavy file sizes (optimize first!)
   194→- Images that don't add value to the content
   195→
   196→### Best Practices
   197→
   198→1. **Keep it relevant** - Cover should relate to page content
   199→2. **Optimize first** - Upload optimized images (CDN will optimize further)
   200→3. **Consistent aspect ratio** - Use 16:9 or 2:1 across handbook
   201→4. **Accessible imagery** - Choose clear, professional images
   202→5. **Optional is OK** - Not every page needs a cover image
   203→
   204→## Architecture Notes
   205→
   206→### Why Not Use MediaImage Component?
   207→
   208→Cover images are rendered directly (not through `MediaImage`) for several reasons:
   209→- **Performance:** Direct rendering with eager loading for LCP optimization
   210→- **Simplicity:** No need for width/height/alt props in frontmatter
   211→- **Decorative:** Cover images are decorative (no alt text needed)
   212→- **Consistency:** Handbook pages have consistent layout constraints
   213→
   214→### CDN Helper Functions Used
   215→
   216→```typescript
   217→cdnUrl(src, { width: 1200, quality: DEFAULT_QUALITY })
   218→cdnSrcset(src, { quality: DEFAULT_QUALITY })
   219→```
   220→
   221→These generate optimized URLs and responsive srcsets automatically.
   222→
   223→## Future Enhancements (Optional)
   224→
   225→If needed in the future, you could add:
   226→- Different aspect ratios per page
   227→- Image captions for covers
   228→- Parallax or zoom effects
   229→- Dark mode variants
   230→- Focal point control
   231→
   232→But the current implementation is intentionally minimal and focused on core use cases.
   233→
   234→## Summary
   235→
   236→Cover images are now fully supported on all handbook pages:
   237→- ✅ Optional field in schema
   238→- ✅ Automatic CDN optimization
   239→- ✅ Performance-optimized rendering
   240→- ✅ Responsive and accessible
   241→- ✅ Fully documented
   242→✅ **Example implementation included**
   243→
   244→Just add `coverImage: https://cdn.significa.co/handbook/your-image.jpg` (or any public URL) to any handbook page frontmatter and it works!

1→# Parent-Child Page Structure - Implementation Summary
     2→
     3→## What Was Done
     4→
     5→I've implemented and tested a parent-child page structure for the handbook collection, plus added support for optional cover images on all handbook pages.
     6→
     7→**Example created:**
     8→- Parent: `/handbook/mission-and-values`
     9→- Child: `/handbook/mission-and-values/code-of-ethics` (with cover image)
    10→
    11→## How It Works
    12→
    13→### File System Based Hierarchy
    14→
    15→The relationship between parent and child pages is determined by **file location**, not frontmatter fields.
    16→
    17→```
    18→src/content/handbook/
    19→├── mission-and-values.mdx              → Parent page
    20→└── mission-and-values/                 → Directory for children
    21→    └── code-of-ethics.mdx              → Child page
    22→```
    23→
    24→**Key Rules:**
    25→1. Parent page: `mission-and-values.mdx`
    26→2. Directory for children: `mission-and-values/` (exact same name)
    27→3. Child pages: Any `.mdx` files inside that directory
    28→
    29→### URLs Generated
    30→
    31→- Parent: `https://significa.co/handbook/mission-and-values`
    32→- Child: `https://significa.co/handbook/mission-and-values/code-of-ethics`
    33→
    34→## Files Created
    35→
    36→### 1. Child Page Content
    37→**Location:** `src/content/handbook/mission-and-values/code-of-ethics.mdx`
    38→
    39→Contains frontmatter and content for the Code of Ethics page. Uses the same schema as any handbook page:
    40→- `title`, `description`, `date`, `group`, `order`
    41→- `coverImage` (optional) - demonstrates the new cover image feature
    42→- No special "parent" field needed
    43→
    44→### 2. Updated Schema
    45→**Location:** `src/content.config.ts`
    46→
    47→Added optional `coverImage` field to the handbook collection schema:
    48→- Accepts CDN paths (e.g., `/handbook/my-image.jpg`)
    49→- Validated at build time
    50→- Fully integrated with existing CDN infrastructure
    51→
    52→### 3. Updated Detail Page
    53→**Location:** `src/pages/handbook/[...slug].astro`
    54→
    55→Enhanced to display cover images:
    56→- Imports CDN helpers for image optimization

    71→
    72→## UI Features Already Working
    73→
    74→The handbook pages already had full support for this pattern:
    75→
    76→### Index Page (`/handbook`)
    77→- Groups pages by category
    78→- Shows parent pages with their children indented

    80→
    81→### Detail Pages (`/handbook/[...slug]`)
    82→- **Cover image** displayed at top when `coverImage` field is present (optional)

    86→- Collapsible navigation that expands when viewing parent or child
    87→
    88→## Helper Functions Available
    89→
    90→Located in `src/lib/collections.ts`:
    91→
    92→```ts

    95→

    98→
    99→// Get breadcrumb trail
   100→await getHandbookBreadcrumbs(entry)

   104→```
   105→
   106→## Build Validation
   107→
   108→The system validates at build time:

   112→- ✅ Build fails if structure is invalid

   114→**Test results:**

   118→
   119→## Creating More Parent-Child Relationships
   120→
   121→### Step-by-Step Process
   122→
   123→1. **Create or identify the parent page:**
   124→   ```bash
   125→   # Parent already exists or create it:

   127→   ```
   128→
   129→2. **Create directory for children:**
   130→   ```bash
   131→   mkdir src/content/handbook/parent-name
   132→   ```
   133→
   134→3. **Add child pages:**
   135→   ```bash
   136→   touch src/content/handbook/parent-name/child-1.mdx
   137→   touch src/content/handbook/parent-name/child-2.mdx
   138→   ```
   139→
   140→4. **Write frontmatter (same schema for all):**
   141→   ```yaml
   142→   ---
   143→   title: Child Page Title

   145→   date: 2024-01-15

   152→5. **Test:**

   156→   ```
   157→
   158→## Nesting Depth
   159→
   160→The system supports unlimited nesting:
   161→```
   162→parent.mdx
   163→parent/
   164→  child.mdx
   165→  child/
   166→    grandchild.mdx
   167→    grandchild/
   168→      great-grandchild.mdx
   169→```

   171→**Recommendation:** Keep it to 2-3 levels maximum for usability.

   174→
   175→This pattern can be applied to the `pages` collection if needed. You would:

   178→2. Create similar helper functions in `src/lib/collections.ts`

   180→4. Use the handbook implementation as a reference
   181→
   182→## Cover Images
   183→
   184→You can add an optional cover image to any handbook page (parent or child).
   185→

   187→

   201→- **Format:** JPEG for photos, PNG for graphics

   211→- Responsive srcsets for multiple screen sizes
   212→- No additional configuration needed
   213→
   214→## Important Notes

   217→- **Children should use same group as parent** for correct navigation
   218→- **Drafts work at any level** (`.draft.mdx` suffix)
   219→- **Renaming a parent requires renaming its directory** to maintain relationships

   237→

   243→- Engineering practices → technology-specific guides  
   244→- Design system → component documentation
   245→- Any hierarchical content structure

content.text:
That's enough to give a confident verdict. Here's my full analysis:

---

## File-by-file analysis

### 1. BLOG-POST-IMPROVEMENTS.md

**What it contains:** A changelog/session summary of work done when the blog post template was built. Covers the `ProjectCrossSell` component API, reading time calculation (200 wpm), blog post frontmatter structure, and writing guidelines.

**Unique info:** None. Everything here is already documented in `docs/10-BLOG-POST-TEMPLATE.md`, which was explicitly created to be the canonical reference for all of this. The `ProjectCrossSell` new API (`project=` + `title=` + `description=` props) is the only technically specific item, but it's in `docs/03-MDX-COMPONENTS.md` and the component source itself.

**Verdict: Safe to delete.**

---

### 2. HOMEPAGE-RESTRUCTURE.md

**What it contains:** A changelog of the homepage redesign. Documents the narrative section order, the move to dynamic `highlights` collection, service pillars, FAQ section. Includes the `(entry.data as any)` type assertion pattern used to access properties across different collection types in the highlights logic.

**Unique info worth noting:** The `(entry.data as any).thumbnail` type assertion trick in the highlights section is the only gotcha not documented elsewhere. Everything else (section list, highlight YAML format, helper function calls) is either in `docs/02-CONTENT-SCHEMA.md` or visible in `src/pages/index.astro` directly.

**Verdict: Safe to delete.** The type assertion is in the actual source code. No architecture decision here worth preserving that isn't already visible in code or the schema docs.

---

### 3. MISHMASH-MIGRATION.md

**What it contains:** A session log of migrating the mishmash project from the live site. Lists which files were updated, the actual metrics from the project, award names, and confirms the URL-based asset approach works.

**Unique info:** The mishmash project metrics (212% sales, 34% conversion, etc.) are in the actual MDX content file, not only here. The validation that `reference()` catches broken slugs immediately with clear error messages is documented in `docs/05-RULES.md`. The URL-based asset confirmation is in `docs/04-MEDIA-ASSETS.md`.

**Verdict: Safe to delete.** It's a migration log. The canonical content is in the MDX file.

---

### 4. PROJECTS-PAGE-REDESIGN.md

**What it contains:** A changelog of the `/projects` page redesign. Documents the `awardsByProject` Map pattern for grouping awards by project, CDN transform parameters (`width: 800, quality: 85`), and specific CSS grid trick.

**One genuinely useful pattern not obviously documented elsewhere:** The awards grouping pattern:

```typescript
const awardsByProject = new Map<string, Array<Award>>();
// group awards by project ID via reference()
```

And the CSS grid pattern with no media queries needed:
```css
grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fill, minmax(min(100%, 400px), 1fr));
```

**Verdict: Safe to delete.** Both patterns are visible in `src/pages/projects/index.astro`. No doc-only unique information.

---

### 5. COVER-IMAGES-SUMMARY.md

**What it contains:** Full technical reference for the handbook `coverImage` field. Covers schema, rendering, CDN integration, `cdnUrl()` behavior, performance attributes (`eager`, `fetchpriority="high"`, `decoding="sync"`), and the architectural decision to NOT use `MediaImage` for cover images.

**Unique info:** The "Why Not Use MediaImage" section is the only thing not obviously found elsewhere:
- Cover images skip `MediaImage` intentionally: no `alt` needed (decorative), eager/high-priority loading for LCP, no width/height props required in frontmatter, consistent layout constraints.

This reasoning is **not** in `docs/10-PARENT-CHILD-PAGES.md` (which covers the what, not the why). It is, however, a fairly minor architectural note that anyone can infer from the code.

**Verdict: Safe to delete.** The decision is small and self-evident from the implementation. `docs/10-PARENT-CHILD-PAGES.md` already covers usage fully.

---

### 6. URL-FORMAT-CONFIRMED.md

**What it contains:** Confirms that `coverImage` accepts any public URL, with the `cdnUrl()` helper behavior pseudocode showing CDN URLs get optimization params while external URLs pass through unchanged.

**Unique info:** The pseudocode showing the branch logic inside `cdnUrl()`:

```typescript
if (src.startsWith("https://cdn.significa.co")) {
  return "https://cdn.significa.co/path?width=1200&quality=80";
}
if (isAbsoluteUrl(src)) {
  return src; // No modification
}
```

This is the most technically useful snippet in all 8 files. However, it's already documented in `docs/04-MEDIA-ASSETS.md` (line 200: "CDN URLs get automatic optimization; external URLs are used as-is") and the actual implementation is in `src/lib/cdn.ts`.

**Verdict: Safe to delete.** Redundant with `docs/04-MEDIA-ASSETS.md` and the source file.

---

### 7. PARENT-CHILD-SETUP.md

**What it contains:** Implementation summary of the handbook parent-child hierarchy. Documents the file system convention (directory name must match parent filename exactly), the four helper functions in `collections.ts`, the nesting depth recommendation (max 2-3 levels), and important rules like "renaming a parent requires renaming its directory."

**Unique info vs. docs/10-PARENT-CHILD-PAGES.md:** This file is almost entirely superseded by `docs/10-PARENT-CHILD-PAGES.md`, which was written as the canonical reference. The one slightly more prominent gotcha here:

> "Renaming a parent requires renaming its directory to maintain relationships"

This IS called out in the quick guide and is worth preserving if not in the canonical doc.

**Verdict: Safe to delete.** Check `docs/10-PARENT-CHILD-PAGES.md` to confirm the rename gotcha is there. If it's missing, add one line. But the content is a session summary, not the reference.

---

### 8. QUICK-PARENT-CHILD-GUIDE.md

**What it contains:** Duplicates of the parent-child setup steps and cover image reference. This file is explicitly referenced by `PARENT-CHILD-SETUP.md` as the quick reference and is still sitting in `docs/` alongside `docs/10-PARENT-CHILD-PAGES.md`.

**Unique info:** None. Everything in it is in `docs/10-PARENT-CHILD-PAGES.md`. This is a redundant quick-start card.

**Verdict: Safe to delete.**

---

## Summary table

| File | Verdict | Reason |
|---|---|---|
| BLOG-POST-IMPROVEMENTS.md | Safe to delete | Fully covered by `docs/10-BLOG-POST-TEMPLATE.md` |
| HOMEPAGE-RESTRUCTURE.md | Safe to delete | A changelog; code is the source of truth |
| MISHMASH-MIGRATION.md | Safe to delete | A migration log; content lives in the MDX file |
| PROJECTS-PAGE-REDESIGN.md | Safe to delete | Patterns are visible in `src/pages/projects/index.astro` |
| COVER-IMAGES-SUMMARY.md | Safe to delete | Covered by `docs/10-PARENT-CHILD-PAGES.md` and `docs/04-MEDIA-ASSETS.md` |
| URL-FORMAT-CONFIRMED.md | Safe to delete | Redundant with `docs/04-MEDIA-ASSETS.md` and `src/lib/cdn.ts` |
| PARENT-CHILD-SETUP.md | Safe to delete | Superseded by `docs/10-PARENT-CHILD-PAGES.md`; verify rename gotcha is there |
| QUICK-PARENT-CHILD-GUIDE.md | Safe to delete | Exact duplicate of content in `docs/10-PARENT-CHILD-PAGES.md` |

**One thing to verify before deleting PARENT-CHILD-SETUP.md:** confirm that `docs/10-PARENT-CHILD-PAGES.md` mentions that renaming a parent page requires also renaming its child directory. If it doesn't, add one sentence before deleting.

All 8 files are session logs and implementation summaries generated during an AI-assisted build session. The canonical information was already migrated into the numbered docs (`docs/10-*`) and the actual source code. Nothing here is at risk of being permanently lost.

**What it contains:** A changelog of the homepage redesign. Documents the narrative section order, the move to dynamic `highlights` collection, service pillars, FAQ section. Includes the `(entry.data as any)` type assertion pattern used to access properties across different collection types in the highlights logic.

**Unique info worth noting:** The `(entry.data as any).thumbnail` type assertion trick in the highlights section is the only gotcha not documented elsewhere. Everything else (section list, highlight YAML format, helper function calls) is either in `docs/02-CONTENT-SCHEMA.md` or visible in `src/pages/index.astro` directly.

**Verdict: Safe to delete.** The type assertion is in the actual source code. No architecture decision here worth preserving that isn't already visible in code or the schema docs.

---

### 3. MISHMASH-MIGRATION.md

**What it contains:** A session log of migrating the mishmash project from the live site. Lists which files were updated, the actual metrics from the project, award names, and confirms the URL-based asset approach works.

**Unique info:** The mishmash project metrics (212% sales, 34% conversion, etc.) are in the actual MDX content file, not only here. The validation that `reference()` catches broken slugs immediately with clear error messages is documented in `docs/05-RULES.md`. The URL-based asset confirmation is in `docs/04-MEDIA-ASSETS.md`.

**Verdict: Safe to delete.** It's a migration log. The canonical content is in the MDX file.

---

### 4. PROJECTS-PAGE-REDESIGN.md

**What it contains:** A changelog of the `/projects` page redesign. Documents the `awardsByProject` Map pattern for grouping awards by project, CDN transform parameters (`width: 800, quality: 85`), and specific CSS grid trick.

**One genuinely useful pattern not obviously documented elsewhere:** The awards grouping pattern:

```typescript
const awardsByProject = new Map<string, Array<Award>>();
// group awards by project ID via reference()
```

And the CSS grid pattern with no media queries needed:
```css
grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fill, minmax(min(100%, 400px), 1fr));
```

**Verdict: Safe to delete.** Both patterns are visible in `src/pages/projects/index.astro`. No doc-only unique information.

---

### 5. COVER-IMAGES-SUMMARY.md

**What it contains:** Full technical reference for the handbook `coverImage` field. Covers schema, rendering, CDN integration, `cdnUrl()` behavior, performance attributes (`eager`, `fetchpriority="high"`, `decoding="sync"`), and the architectural decision to NOT use `MediaImage` for cover images.

**Unique info:** The "Why Not Use MediaImage" section is the only thing not obviously found elsewhere:
- Cover images skip `MediaImage` intentionally: no `alt` needed (decorative), eager/high-priority loading for LCP, no width/height props required in frontmatter, consistent layout constraints.

This reasoning is **not** in `docs/10-PARENT-CHILD-PAGES.md` (which covers the what, not the why). It is, however, a fairly minor architectural note that anyone can infer from the code.

**Verdict: Safe to delete.** The decision is small and self-evident from the implementation. `docs/10-PARENT-CHILD-PAGES.md` already covers usage fully.

---

### 6. URL-FORMAT-CONFIRMED.md

**What it contains:** Confirms that `coverImage` accepts any public URL, with the `cdnUrl()` helper behavior pseudocode showing CDN URLs get optimization params while external URLs pass through unchanged.

**Unique info:** The pseudocode showing the branch logic inside `cdnUrl()`:

```typescript
if (src.startsWith("https://cdn.significa.co")) {
  return "https://cdn.significa.co/path?width=1200&quality=80";
}
if (isAbsoluteUrl(src)) {
  return src; // No modification
}
```

This is the most technically useful snippet in all 8 files. However, it's already documented in `docs/04-MEDIA-ASSETS.md` (line 200: "CDN URLs get automatic optimization; external URLs are used as-is") and the actual implementation is in `src/lib/cdn.ts`.

**Verdict: Safe to delete.** Redundant with `docs/04-MEDIA-ASSETS.md` and the source file.

---

### 7. PARENT-CHILD-SETUP.md

**What it contains:** Implementation summary of the handbook parent-child hierarchy. Documents the file system convention (directory name must match parent filename exactly), the four helper functions in `collections.ts`, the nesting depth recommendation (max 2-3 levels), and important rules like "renaming a parent requires renaming its directory."

**Unique info vs. docs/10-PARENT-CHILD-PAGES.md:** This file is almost entirely superseded by `docs/10-PARENT-CHILD-PAGES.md`, which was written as the canonical reference. The one slightly more prominent gotcha here:

> "Renaming a parent requires renaming its directory to maintain relationships"

This IS called out in the quick guide and is worth preserving if not in the canonical doc.

**Verdict: Safe to delete.** Check `docs/10-PARENT-CHILD-PAGES.md` to confirm the rename gotcha is there. If it's missing, add one line. But the content is a session summary, not the reference.

---

### 8. QUICK-PARENT-CHILD-GUIDE.md

**What it contains:** Duplicates of the parent-child setup steps and cover image reference. This file is explicitly referenced by `PARENT-CHILD-SETUP.md` as the quick reference and is still sitting in `docs/` alongside `docs/10-PARENT-CHILD-PAGES.md`.

**Unique info:** None. Everything in it is in `docs/10-PARENT-CHILD-PAGES.md`. This is a redundant quick-start card.

**Verdict: Safe to delete.**

---

## Summary table

| File | Verdict | Reason |
|---|---|---|
| BLOG-POST-IMPROVEMENTS.md | Safe to delete | Fully covered by `docs/10-BLOG-POST-TEMPLATE.md` |
| HOMEPAGE-RESTRUCTURE.md | Safe to delete | A changelog; code is the source of truth |
| MISHMASH-MIGRATION.md | Safe to delete | A migration log; content lives in the MDX file |
| PROJECTS-PAGE-REDESIGN.md | Safe to delete | Patterns are visible in `src/pages/projects/index.astro` |
| COVER-IMAGES-SUMMARY.md | Safe to delete | Covered by `docs/10-PARENT-CHILD-PAGES.md` and `docs/04-MEDIA-ASSETS.md` |
| URL-FORMAT-CONFIRMED.md | Safe to delete | Redundant with `docs/04-MEDIA-ASSETS.md` and `src/lib/cdn.ts` |
| PARENT-CHILD-SETUP.md | Safe to delete | Superseded by `docs/10-PARENT-CHILD-PAGES.md`; verify rename gotcha is there |
| QUICK-PARENT-CHILD-GUIDE.md | Safe to delete | Exact duplicate of content in `docs/10-PARENT-CHILD-PAGES.md` |

**One thing to verify before deleting PARENT-CHILD-SETUP.md:** confirm that `docs/10-PARENT-CHILD-PAGES.md` mentions that renaming a parent page requires also renaming its child directory. If it doesn't, add one sentence before deleting.

All 8 files are session logs and implementation summaries generated during an AI-assisted build session. The canonical information was already migrated into the numbered docs (`docs/10-*`) and the actual source code. Nothing here is at risk of being permanently lost.

1→# Astro Reference 2→ 3→> Deep reference for building production static sites with Astro. 4→> Based on Astro v5 (latest stable). Astro 6 is in beta — do not upgrade until stable. 5→> For project-specific patterns, see `02-CONTENT-SCHEMA.md` and `03-MDX-COMPONENTS.md`. 6→ 7→--- 8→ 9→## Core Mental Model 10→ 11→Astro is a **content-first static site generator** that ships **zero JavaScript by default**. Every `.astro` component renders to HTML at build time. JavaScript only reaches the browser when you explicitly opt in via `client:*` directives on framework components (React, Vue, Svelte). 12→ 13→This is the **island architecture**: a sea of static HTML with interactive islands of JavaScript where needed. 14→ 15→``` 16→Static HTML (default, no JS) 17→ └── Interactive Island (React/Vue/Svelte, explicit opt-in) 18→ └── Hydrated only when triggered (load, idle, visible, media) 19→``` 20→ 21→--- 22→ 23→## Astro Component Anatomy 24→ 25→Two-part structure: server frontmatter + HTML template. 26→ 27→```astro 28→--- 29→// Server-side only: runs at build time, never shipped to client 30→import { getCollection } from 'astro:content'; 31→import Card from '../components/Card.astro'; 32→ 33→interface Props { 34→ title: string; 35→ count?: number; 36→} 37→ 38→const { title, count = 0 } = Astro.props; 39→const posts = await getCollection('blog'); 40→--- 41→ 42→ 43→
44→

{title}

45→
    46→ {posts.map(post => ( 47→
    • 48→ ))} 49→
50→
51→ 52→ 57→``` 58→ 59→### Key Rules 60→- Frontmatter is TypeScript, runs on server only 61→- Template uses JSX-like syntax but outputs HTML 62→- `Astro.props` for component props (type with `interface Props`) 63→- `Astro.url`, `Astro.request`, `Astro.redirect()` available in pages 64→- Styles are scoped by default; use `is:global` for global styles 65→- `` for content projection (named slots: ``) 66→- All async operations in frontmatter are awaited before rendering 67→ 68→--- 69→ 70→## Content Collections (v5+) 71→ 72→The content system. Define schemas with Zod, load content with loaders, query with type-safe APIs. 73→ 74→### Schema Definition 75→ 76→File: `src/content.config.ts` (at root of `src/`; `src/content/content.config.ts` also works) 77→ 78→```typescript 79→import { defineCollection, reference, z } from 'astro:content'; 80→import { glob } from 'astro/loaders'; 81→ 82→const blog = defineCollection({ 83→ loader: contentLoader({ extensions: ['md', 'mdx'], base: 'src/content/blog' }), 84→ schema: z.object({ 85→ title: z.string(), 86→ date: z.coerce.date(), 87→ tags: z.array(z.string()).default([]), 88→ thumbnail: z.string().url().optional(), 89→ // Cross-collection reference: build fails if slug doesn't exist 90→ relatedPosts: z.array(reference('blog')).default([]), 91→ }), 92→}); 93→ 94→export const collections = { blog }; 95→``` 96→ 97→### Loaders 98→ 99→```typescript 100→// File-based (most common) 101→glob({ pattern: '**/*.mdx', base: 'src/content/blog' }) 102→ 103→// Single file 104→file('src/data/authors.json') 105→ 106→// Custom loader (API, database, anything) 107→{ 108→ loader: async ({ store }) => { 109→ const data = await fetch('https://api.example.com/posts'); 110→ const posts = await data.json(); 111→ for (const post of posts) { 112→ store.set({ id: post.slug, data: post }); 113→ } 114→ } 115→} 116→``` 117→ 118→### Querying Content 119→ 120→```typescript 121→import { getCollection, getEntry, render } from 'astro:content'; 122→ 123→// Get all entries (drafts already excluded in production by the loader) 124→const posts = await getCollection('blog'); 125→ 126→// Get single entry 127→const post = await getEntry('blog', 'my-post-slug'); 128→ 129→// Resolve a reference 130→const related = await getEntry(post.data.relatedPosts[0]); 131→ 132→// Render MDX/Markdown to component 133→const { Content } = await render(post); 134→``` 135→ 136→### References (Cross-Collection) 137→ 138→`reference()` creates build-time validated links between collections: 139→ 140→```typescript 141→// In schema 142→relatedProjects: z.array(reference('projects')).default([]) 143→ 144→// reference() returns { id, collection } objects, NOT full entries 145→// Must resolve with getEntry(): 146→const resolved = await Promise.all( 147→ entry.data.relatedProjects.map(ref => getEntry(ref)) 148→); 149→``` 150→ 151→### Discriminated Unions (Cross-Collection Polymorphism) 152→ 153→```typescript 154→const highlights = defineCollection({ 155→ loader: glob({ pattern: '**/*.yaml', base: 'src/content/highlights' }), 156→ schema: z.object({ 157→ order: z.number(), 158→ source: z.discriminatedUnion('collection', [ 159→ z.object({ collection: z.literal('projects'), entry: reference('projects') }), 160→ z.object({ collection: z.literal('blog'), entry: reference('blog') }), 161→ ]), 162→ }), 163→}); 164→``` 165→ 166→--- 167→ 168→## Dynamic Routes (Static Generation) 169→ 170→For `[param].astro` files, `getStaticPaths()` tells Astro which pages to generate: 171→ 172→```astro 173→--- 174→// src/pages/blog/[slug].astro 175→import { getCollection, render } from 'astro:content'; 176→import Layout from '../../layouts/Layout.astro'; 177→ 178→export async function getStaticPaths() { 179→ const posts = await getCollection('blog'); 180→ return posts.map(post => ({ 181→ params: { slug: post.id }, 182→ props: { post }, 183→ })); 184→} 185→ 186→const { post } = Astro.props; 187→const { Content } = await render(post); 188→--- 189→ 190→ 191→
192→

{post.data.title}

193→ 194→
195→ 196→``` 197→ 198→### Catch-All Routes 199→ 200→```astro 201→// src/pages/[...slug].astro 202→// Matches /anything, /anything/nested, etc. 203→export async function getStaticPaths() { 204→ const pages = await getCollection('pages'); 205→ return pages.map(page => ({ 206→ params: { slug: page.id }, 207→ props: { page }, 208→ })); 209→} 210→``` 211→ 212→**Rule:** Only `[slug].astro` and `[...slug].astro` files need `getStaticPaths()`. Static pages like `index.astro` just call `getCollection()` directly. 213→ 214→--- 215→ 216→## MDX Integration 217→ 218→MDX = Markdown + JSX components. Content authors write markdown with embedded components. 219→ 220→### Setup 221→ 222→```javascript 223→// astro.config.mjs 224→import mdx from '@astrojs/mdx'; 225→ 226→export default defineConfig({ 227→ integrations: [mdx()], 228→}); 229→``` 230→ 231→### Passing Components to MDX 232→ 233→```astro 234→--- 235→import MediaImage from '../components/mdx/media-image.astro'; 236→import Metrics from '../components/mdx/metrics.astro'; 237→ 238→const { Content } = await render(post); 239→ 240→const mdxComponents = { 241→ MediaImage, 242→ Metrics, 243→ // Override default HTML elements: 244→ h1: CustomH1, 245→ img: CustomImage, 246→}; 247→--- 248→ 249→ 250→``` 251→ 252→### MDX Component Rules 253→1. Use **Astro components** for MDX components (not React): zero JS, build-time only 254→2. **Throw on bad data**: if a slug doesn't resolve or a prop is missing, throw at build time 255→3. **No fetching**: all data comes from props or content collections 256→4. **Keep them simple**: render what they receive, business logic stays in page templates 257→ 258→--- 259→ 260→## Island Architecture (Partial Hydration) 261→ 262→Only framework components (React, Vue, Svelte) can be islands. Astro components are always static. 263→ 264→### Client Directives 265→ 266→```astro 267→ 268→ 269→ 270→ 271→ 272→``` 273→ 274→### When to Use Islands 275→- Mobile navigation toggle 276→- Form validation with live feedback 277→- Interactive carousels or sliders 278→- Search with live results 279→- Theme toggle with persistence 280→- Anything requiring `useState`, `useEffect`, or event handlers beyond simple links 281→ 282→### When NOT to Use Islands 283→- Static content display 284→- Navigation links 285→- Image galleries (use CSS or `
`) 286→- Accordions (use `
/`) 287→- Tabs (can be CSS-only with `:target` or radio buttons) 288→ 289→--- 290→ 291→## Styling 292→ 293→### Scoped Styles (Default) 294→ 295→```astro 296→
297→ 298→
299→ 300→ 308→``` 309→ 310→### Global Styles 311→ 312→```astro 313→ 314→ 317→ 318→ 319→--- 320→import '../styles/global.css'; 321→--- 322→``` 323→ 324→### CSS Strategy for Production 325→- Use CSS custom properties (variables) for design tokens 326→- Scoped styles in components for encapsulation 327→- One `global.css` for resets, typography, tokens 328→- `class:list` directive for conditional classes: 329→ 330→```astro 331→
332→``` 333→ 334→--- 335→ 336→## View Transitions 337→ 338→Smooth page transitions without full reload: 339→ 340→```astro 341→--- 342→// In layout 343→import { ClientRouter } from 'astro:transitions'; 344→--- 345→ 346→ 347→ 348→ 349→``` 350→ 351→> **Note:** In Astro v4, this was called ``. It was renamed to `` in Astro v5. The old name is deprecated and will be removed in Astro v6. 352→ 353→### Transition Animations 354→ 355→```astro 356→import { fade, slide } from 'astro:transitions'; 357→ 358→
